user_query:
I’m organizing a small, positive outreach push as a local environmentalist in Rafter’s constituency and need a compact content plan built around one reputable, openly licensed community action resource.

Please do the following and keep everything inside this workspace:

1) Download an official, openly licensed community environmental action toolkit PDF from the Internet and save it to downloads/toolkit.pdf. The document must:
- Be a PDF published by an official public authority (.gov, .eu, .int) or a recognized NGO (.org) and clearly intended for community action (e.g., a “toolkit” or “guide”).
- Focus on at least one of these topics: recycling, clean air, water stewardship, or tree planting.
- Use an open license (e.g., Creative Commons, Open Government Licence). If the license is not clearly discoverable in the PDF’s properties or first pages, treat it as unknown.
Do not include the direct download URL in this request, but you must record it in metadata once you find it.

2) Create downloads/toolkit_meta.json with this exact JSON schema (strings unless noted):
{
  "source_url": "...",
  "title": "...",
  "page_count": <integer>,
  "license": "...",
  "retrieved_at": "<ISO-8601 timestamp>"
}
- title: prefer the PDF’s document title property; if absent, use the filename without the extension.
- page_count: count pages from the PDF.
- license: the exact license string if you can identify it from the PDF; otherwise "unknown".
- retrieved_at: the timestamp when you downloaded it.

3) Modify config/campaign_config.yaml (already provided) to add these new keys at the root level and save the updated file in place:
- featured_resource:
    path: "downloads/toolkit.pdf"
    title: <from downloads/toolkit_meta.json>
    page_count: <from downloads/toolkit_meta.json>
- email_subject_prefix: "[Rafter Constituency Green Action]"
- hashtags: an array derived from preferred_topics by removing spaces and prepending a # (e.g., "clean rivers" -> "#CleanRivers").
Do not remove existing keys. Keep constituency_name as-is ("Rafter’s constituency").

4) Draft tailored outreach emails (do not send) for each group in input/community_contacts.csv using templates/outreach_email.txt. For each row, create a file:
- Path: output/emails/<slugified-group-name>.txt (lowercase, hyphens for spaces, alphanumerics and hyphens only)
- Replace these placeholders in the template:
  - {{group_name}}: from CSV
  - {{constituency_name}}: from config
  - {{preferred_topics_list}}: comma-separated list from config.preferred_topics
  - {{email_subject_prefix}}: from config
  - {{resource_summary}}: “<title> — <page_count> pages — saved at downloads/toolkit.pdf” using values from downloads/toolkit_meta.json
Ensure the subject line in the draft matches the template substitution.

5) Build a compact social content plan at output/social_posts.json with exactly one post per date listed in config.planned_dates. Each post must be a JSON object with keys:
- date: the date from planned_dates
- channel: one from allowed_channels excluding "email"
- topic: one value from preferred_topics
- text: a short message (<= 280 chars) that mentions constituency_name and includes at least one of the config.hashtags and references the featured resource by name (title) without external links
- resource_path: "downloads/toolkit.pdf"
Use only values sourced from the updated config and toolkit_meta.json. No external links.

Inputs you must use:
- config/campaign_config.yaml (edit in place as above)
- input/community_contacts.csv
- templates/outreach_email.txt

Deliverables:
- downloads/toolkit.pdf (downloaded from an official authority or recognized NGO as described)
- downloads/toolkit_meta.json (schema exactly as specified)
- config/campaign_config.yaml (updated with featured_resource, email_subject_prefix, hashtags)
- output/emails/*.txt (one per group from CSV with placeholders resolved)
- output/social_posts.json (one post per planned date, fields and constraints as above)

Please do not make up resource data; obtain and record it from the downloaded PDF. Keep paths exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
